titlestmt.titleproper: International Congress on the History of Sciences records14-17-744
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-11-01 23:01:59 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: International Congress on the History of Sciences records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: International Congress on the History of Sciences

did.physdesc: Correspondence, abstracts, papers, periodicals, mailing lists, bulletins, minutes, memoranda, reports, tape recordings, and other material.
did.abstract: Correspondence relating to the planning, financing, and publicity for the Congress held at Ithaca, New York, and Philadelphia in August and September, 1962.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence relating to the planning, financing, and publicity for the Congress held at Ithaca, New York, and Philadelphia in August and September, 1962; also includes lists of participants, copies of abstracts and papers presented with commentaries, periodicals, and mailing lists; also, drafts and final copies of the Congress' bulletins, minutes and memoranda of the planning and local arrangement committees, reports, and other material. Also, tape recordings of the proceedings. Henry Guerlac was the president of the Congress, Richard Shryock chaired the Philadelphia operations.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: International Congress of the History of Science records, #14-17-744. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Congresses and conventions -- Pennsylvania -- Philadelphia.
Congresses and conventions -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Science -- History.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Series I. Correspondence, Pamphlets, and Conference Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chart of names, vouchers, grants, refunds, remarks, etc. Section 10, The New York Times - The Nation's No. 1 Shrine Philadelphia Official Guide Post Assembly Technical Tours, June 1958. Cards - Names, Attendances or no - payments. (approx. 150) Gard - John Anthony McVickar - Soviet and Eastern European Exchange Staff. U.S. Dept. of State, Washington E.G. - DU J-6120

did.unittitle: Twelve Scripts of Addresses delivered at the Congress

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Of the twelve addresses, six deal with Symposium IV, Topic: "The Discovery of the Neutron and its Effects on Physics"; while the other six deal with general topics given throughout the congress. Unpublished guide follows.

did.unittitle: II.b. Symposium IV: Six Scripts on Topic of "The Discovery of the Neutron and its Effects Upon Physics: A case Study"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Symposium IV held at Cornell University August 31, 1962

did.unittitle: 1. Nuclear Physics Without The Neutron: Clues and Contradictions.
